description: CT Pulmonary Angiogram (chest, with IV contrast)
facility: University of Maryland Medical Center — Emergency Radiology
indication: Acute pleuritic chest pain and dyspnea; evaluate for pulmonary embolism.
technique: CT pulmonary angiography, pulmonary arterial phase, with axial/coronal/sagittal reformats. The upper abdomen is partially included.
findings:
PULMONARY ARTERIES: Filling defect consistent with a SEGMENTAL pulmonary embolus in the right lower lobe segmental arteries. No central, saddle, or main-pulmonary-artery embolus. Right-ventricle-to-left-ventricle (RV:LV) ratio is normal; no CT signs of right-heart strain. No infarction.

LUNGS/PLEURA: No consolidation. Small right pleural-based wedge opacity in the lower lobe (possible early infarct vs atelectasis). No effusion.

PARTIALLY IMAGED UPPER ABDOMEN: Heterogeneous enhancement in the right lobe of the liver is noted at the inferior edge of the field of view. This most likely represents focal fatty infiltration; however, a mass — including primary or metastatic malignancy — cannot be excluded. Dedicated liver imaging is recommended for characterization.
impression:
1. Acute SEGMENTAL pulmonary embolism, right lower lobe. No CT evidence of right-heart strain (low-risk).
2. Incidental heterogeneous right-hepatic-lobe enhancement — favored to be focal fatty infiltration, but malignancy (primary or metastatic) is in the differential. RECOMMEND dedicated liver MRI.
